executive_summary: The session involved the player's initial entry into Night City, covering the opening cinematic, the selection of the Corpo lifepath, and an extensive character customization process. Overall engagement was very high, driven by the player's strong resonance with the game's aesthetic and music, leading to deep immersion in crafting a specific 'hacker' persona.

key_findings.evidence_summary: At 0:52, a prolonged black screen with only a tiny loading icon caused the player to vocally worry, 'Is my computer breaking? This isn't good.' Quality agents confirmed this as a performance-related friction point.
key_findings.finding: Extended static black loading screens without progress feedback cause immediate hardware anxiety and negative sentiment early in the experience.
key_findings.recommended_action: Add a progress bar or dynamic background elements to the initial engine loading transition to confirm the game hasn't crashed.

key_findings.evidence_summary: At 4:40, the player cycled through 18 ear presets but could not see changes because the camera remained front-facing. She noted, 'I can't even see my ears really,' and tilted her own head to try and compensate.
key_findings.finding: Static camera positioning during the selection of side-facing features (ears, side tattoos) creates a disconnect between menu interaction and visual feedback.
key_findings.recommended_action: Implement a contextual camera snap that automatically rotates the character model to a profile view when side-of-head customization categories are selected.

key_findings.evidence_summary: At 8:30 and again at 9:03, the player expressed confusion over 'Accent Color' and 'Blemish Color' sliders. Quote: 'What is up with that accent color?' while the slider appeared to have no effect on certain styles.
key_findings.finding: The UI fails to explain dependencies between primary styles and secondary color sliders, leading to confusion when inputs result in zero visual change.
key_findings.recommended_action: Add tooltips explaining that 'Accent Color' affects metallic sheen or secondary tints, and disable/hide color sliders that do not apply to the current selected style.

key_findings.evidence_summary: The player reached peak delight (Sentiment 10.0) at the end of the session (9:30-9:55) when discovering granular body customization. Her reaction shifted from 'Chrome teeth is a vibe' (6:28) to high-energy laughter at anatomical details.
key_findings.finding: Granular, unexpected customization options like chrome teeth and anatomical details act as significant 'surprise and delight' drivers that strongly reinforce the game's edgy 'punk' identity.
key_findings.recommended_action: Protect the depth of these 'non-standard' customization options as they are primary drivers for player social sharing and character investment.

priority_actions: Add animated loading assets or a percentage bar to the 0:52 transition to mitigate hardware anxiety.
Enable auto-rotation for the character creator camera when selecting side-of-head features like ears.
Clarify secondary color slider functions (Accent/Blemish) through better labeling or conditional UI visibility.
Verify that high-fidelity textures, which the player praised at 5:01, remain consistent across all customization sub-menus.
cross_dimensional_insight: The player's early technical anxiety at 0:52 was entirely neutralized by the visual fidelity of the Westbrook cinematic at 2:14, suggesting that for high-intent players, extreme 'visual payoffs' can act as an emotional reset button, effectively erasing previous minor technical friction.
